items.label: Weather Comments e.g Hot, Windy, Rain

responses.text:
Today, the team began erosion control and vegetation management works at Rotary Cowpasture Reserve. The focus was on reducing invasive species and preparing the site for improved native regeneration while minimizing soil disturbance in erosion-prone areas.

The day commenced with brushcutting dense patches of kikuyu grass across the site. This was done to reduce competition with native species and to allow for more effective herbicide application. Once brushcutting was complete, the team followed up with targeted spraying of kikuyu regrowth to ensure longer-term suppression.

Following this, the team moved through Sites 1, 2, and 3 to carry out selective weeding. A combination of hand weeding and spot spraying was used to remove various environmental weeds while carefully avoiding native grasses and any previous plantings to preserve site integrity and encourage natural revegetation.

The team also focused on the removal of invasive woody weeds, including small privets, olives, and Gleditsia. Smaller individuals were cut and removed using chainsaws. Larger specimens—those thicker than wrist size or identified as providing short-term erosion control—were frilled and herbicide-treated rather than removed, in order to limit soil disturbance and bank destabilisation.
